preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial fricative [ɸ] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a process of voicing and vocalization (lenition). The friction is lost as the articulators move further apart, creating a sonorous glide.

examples.note: In rapid speech, the bilabial friction can reduce to a light glide quality.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
